articles.headline: C. P. Radhakrishnan Elected India's New Vice President
articles.explainer:
• C. P. Radhakrishnan, former Governor of Jharkhand, has been elected as the 15th Vice President of India, securing a significant majority in the electoral college.
• The election involved members of both Houses of Parliament, with Rajya Sabha Secretary-General serving as the Returning Officer.
• The Vice President holds the constitutional office of Chairman of the Rajya Sabha and acts as the President in case of a vacancy.
• Radhakrishnan's victory marks a significant political development, consolidating the ruling party's position in key constitutional offices.
• His tenure is expected to focus on parliamentary decorum and fostering constructive debates within the upper house.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: C. P. Radhakrishnan is the 15th Vice President of India.
The Vice President is elected by members of an electoral college consisting of members of both Houses of Parliament.
The election is held by secret ballot, using the system of proportional representation by means of the single transferable vote.
The Vice President is ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.
Article 63 to 71 of the Indian Constitution deal with the office of the Vice President.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional role and significance of the Vice President of India in the parliamentary system.
Analyze the electoral process for the Vice President and its distinction from the Presidential election.
Evaluate the potential impact of the new Vice President on the functioning of the Rajya Sabha and inter-house relations.
Examine the powers and functions of the Vice President, particularly as the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha and acting President.
articles.imageDescription: C. P. Radhakrishnan taking the oath of office as Vice President.
articles.trivia: The first Vice President of India, Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan, served two terms, from 1952 to 1962.

articles.headline: BCCI Announces India Squad for Upcoming 2025 Asia Cup Tournament
articles.explainer:
• The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) has unveiled the 15-member squad for the 2025 Asia Cup, scheduled to commence on September 28.
• The selection committee emphasized a blend of experienced players and promising young talent, aiming for strong performance in the regional championship.
• The Asia Cup serves as a crucial preparatory tournament for the upcoming ICC Men's T20 World Cup, providing valuable match practice against top Asian teams.
• Key players like Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli, and Jasprit Bumrah are included, alongside new faces expected to make an impact.
• The tournament will be hosted in Sri Lanka, with matches played across Colombo and Kandy, promising exciting contests.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of sports in nation building and promoting cultural exchange.
prelimsPoints: The 2025 Asia Cup is scheduled to begin on September 28.
The tournament is an international cricket competition contested by men's teams from Asia.
The format of the Asia Cup alternates between ODI and T20I depending on the format of the upcoming major ICC event.
BCCI is the governing body for cricket in India.
articles.imageDescription: Indian cricket team players in a huddle during a practice session.
articles.trivia: The Asia Cup is the only continental championship in cricket and is typically held every two years.

articles.headline: New 'PM-Suraksha Awas Yojana' Launched to Boost Affordable Housing
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government has launched the 'PM-Suraksha Awas Yojana' (PMSAY) aimed at providing affordable and quality housing for all eligible urban and rural poor.
• The scheme integrates various existing housing initiatives and introduces new incentives for private sector participation in affordable housing projects.
• Key features include interest subvention on home loans, direct financial assistance for construction, and promotion of sustainable building technologies.
• PMSAY targets particularly vulnerable groups including EWS, LIG, and those living in informal settlements, with a focus on women beneficiaries.
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs will oversee the implementation, working with state governments and local bodies to achieve housing targets by 2030.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: PM-Suraksha Awas Yojana (PMSAY) aims to provide affordable housing.
The scheme covers both urban and rural poor.
It includes provisions for interest subvention and direct financial assistance.
The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is the nodal ministry.
Focus on sustainable building technologies and women beneficiaries.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of affordable housing schemes like PMSAY in achieving inclusive growth and reducing urban poverty.
Discuss the challenges in implementing large-scale housing projects in India and suggest measures for effective delivery.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and technological innovations in accelerating housing construction under such schemes.
Examine how PMSAY addresses the housing needs of vulnerable sections and promotes gender equality in home ownership.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting affordable housing units under a government scheme.
articles.trivia: India aims to achieve 'Housing for All' by a specific target year, which has been a recurring goal in national policies.

articles.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Emission Targets at G20 Climate Forum
articles.explainer:
• India has announced a significant enhancement of its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) at the G20 Climate and Environment Ministers' Meeting.
• The revised targets include a more ambitious reduction in carbon emission intensity and a substantial increase in non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
• The commitment underscores India's dedication to global climate action while balancing its development imperatives and energy security needs.
• India called for greater financial and technological support from developed nations to assist developing countries in their climate mitigation and adaptation efforts.
• Discussions also focused on collective efforts towards achieving net-zero emissions, climate finance, and technology transfer mechanisms.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: India updated its NDCs at the G20 Climate Forum.
NDCs are national climate action plans under the Paris Agreement.
The new targets include reducing carbon emission intensity and increasing non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
G20 is an international forum for the governments and central bank governors from 19 countries and the European Union.
India emphasizes common but differentiated responsibilities and respective capabilities (CBDR-RC).
mainsPoints: Analyze India's enhanced NDCs in the context of global climate action and its implications for sustainable development.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in transitioning to a low-carbon economy, considering its energy security needs.
Evaluate the role of international forums like G20 in fostering climate cooperation and the need for equitable climate finance.
Examine the concept of 'common but differentiated responsibilities' (CBDR-RC) and its relevance in current climate negotiations.
articles.imageDescription: Ministers from G20 nations attending a climate discussion, with flags in the background.
articles.trivia: The Paris Agreement aims to limit global warming to well below 2, preferably to 1.5 degrees Celsius, compared to pre-industrial levels.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Prototype
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has successfully conducted a critical test flight of its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
• This test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating the return of a spacecraft from orbit, a key step towards fully reusable launch systems.
• RLV technology aims to significantly reduce the cost of space access by making rockets reusable, similar to aircraft.
• The development is crucial for India's ambitious future space missions, including human spaceflight and commercial satellite launches.
• ISRO is focusing on indigenization of advanced space technologies to enhance India's self-reliance in the global space industry.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested a Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
RLV technology aims to reduce space launch costs by making rockets reusable.
The test involved a controlled landing experiment.
ISRO is India's national space agency.
This technology is vital for future missions like Gaganyaan (human spaceflight).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its economic implications.
Analyze how indigenization of advanced space technologies contributes to India's self-reliance and global standing in the space sector.
Evaluate the challenges and future prospects of developing fully reusable launch systems and their potential impact on space exploration.
Examine the role of ISRO in driving technological innovation and its contribution to national development.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic illustration of a Reusable Launch Vehicle descending for landing.
articles.trivia: ISRO's first experimental RLV, the Hypersonic Flight Experiment (HEX), was successfully tested in 2016.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive meeting.
• This decision comes as retail inflation shows signs of moderation, moving closer to the RBI's comfort zone, supported by favorable monsoon and supply-side measures.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Economic growth projections remain robust, with expectations of sustained momentum in manufacturing and services sectors.
• Analysts suggest this pause allows the RBI to assess the durability of disinflation and global economic uncertainties before making further moves.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC kept the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
The decision is based on easing retail inflation.
MPC consists of six members (three from RBI, three external).
The primary objective of monetary policy is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for inflation and economic growth.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee in balancing inflation control with the objective of supporting economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in managing inflationary pressures and ensuring financial stability in the Indian economy.
Examine how global economic factors and domestic supply-side dynamics influence the RBI's monetary policy decisions.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) logo with currency notes in the background.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 to bring transparency and accountability in formulating monetary policy.

articles.headline: India and France Deepen Strategic Partnership with New Defence & Tech Pacts
articles.explainer:
• India and France have signed a series of agreements to further strengthen their strategic partnership, with a focus on defense, space, and emerging technologies.
• The pacts include joint development projects in advanced defense manufacturing, collaboration in quantum computing, and enhanced maritime security cooperation in the Indo-Pacific.
• This deepening of ties underscores the shared vision of both nations for a stable, secure, and prosperous global order, particularly in the face of evolving geopolitical challenges.
• Both leaders emphasized the importance of multilateralism and adherence to international law, reiterating their commitment to addressing global issues like climate change and terrorism.
• The agreements are expected to boost bilateral trade, investment, and technological exchange, fostering innovation and economic growth.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and France signed new agreements on defence, space, and technology.
Cooperation focuses on advanced defence manufacturing and quantum computing.
Maritime security in the Indo-Pacific is a key area of collaboration.
France is a key strategic partner for India.
Both nations uphold multilateralism and international law.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of the India-France strategic partnership and its significance in India's foreign policy.
Discuss the key areas of cooperation between India and France, particularly in defence, space, and emerging technologies, and their mutual benefits.
Evaluate the role of this partnership in promoting stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region amidst geopolitical shifts.
Examine how bilateral ties with France contribute to India's technological advancement and strategic autonomy.
articles.imageDescription: Prime Minister of India and President of France shaking hands after signing agreements.
articles.trivia: India and France established a strategic partnership in 1998, a unique relationship among India's global partners.

articles.headline: Integrated Border Management System Pilot Project Launched in Northeast
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs has launched a pilot project for an Integrated Border Management System (IBMS) along a vulnerable stretch of India's northeastern border.
• The system employs advanced surveillance technologies, including drones, thermal imagers, ground sensors, and radar, to enhance real-time monitoring and threat detection.
• The initiative aims to curb illegal infiltration, smuggling, and cross-border crimes, bolstering national security and border integrity.
• IBMS integrates data from various sources into a common operating picture, enabling quicker response times and better coordination among border guarding forces.
• This pilot project will be evaluated for scalability and potential rollout across other challenging border areas of the country.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security.
prelimsPoints: An Integrated Border Management System (IBMS) pilot project launched in the Northeast.
IBMS uses technologies like drones, thermal imagers, and ground sensors.
Aims to curb illegal infiltration, smuggling, and cross-border crimes.
Ministry of Home Affairs is the nodal ministry.
The project seeks to enhance real-time monitoring and coordination among border forces.
mainsPoints: Discuss the need for an Integrated Border Management System (IBMS) in India, particularly in challenging terrains like the Northeast.
Analyze how technological advancements can enhance border security and address various internal security challenges.
Evaluate the effectiveness of IBMS in curbing illegal cross-border activities and improving the coordination among border guarding forces.
Examine the challenges in implementing and scaling up such advanced systems across India's diverse border regions.
articles.imageDescription: A border post with surveillance cameras and drones flying overhead.
articles.trivia: India has a total land border of over 15,000 kilometers, shared with seven countries.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Privacy as Fundamental Right in Data Protection Case
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India has once again affirmed the Right to Privacy as an intrinsic part of the fundamental rights guaranteed under Article 21 of the Constitution, in a significant data protection case.
• The ruling emphasizes the need for a robust data protection framework that balances individual privacy with legitimate state interests and technological advancements.
• This judgment reinforces the principles laid down in the landmark Puttaswamy judgment, providing further clarity on the scope and limitations of privacy in the digital age.
• The court directed the government to expedite the enactment of a comprehensive data protection law, ensuring data fiduciary accountability and individual consent mechanisms.
• The decision has wide-ranging implications for digital governance, surveillance practices, and the collection and processing of personal data by both state and private entities.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Fundamental Rights, Directive Principles and Fundamental Duties.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court affirmed Right to Privacy as part of fundamental rights under Article 21.
The ruling reinforces the Puttaswamy judgment (2017).
The court called for a robust data protection law.
It emphasizes individual consent and data fiduciary accountability.
Implications for digital governance and surveillance practices.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right in India and the significance of the latest Supreme Court ruling in this context.
Analyze the challenges in balancing individual privacy rights with legitimate state interests and the demands of national security in the digital era.
Evaluate the essential features of a comprehensive data protection law for India and how it can safeguard citizens' digital rights.
Examine the impact of such judicial pronouncements on digital governance, surveillance, and the responsibilities of data fiduciaries.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal books, symbolizing judicial pronouncements.
articles.trivia: The K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared that the Right to Privacy is protected as an intrinsic part of the right to life and personal liberty under Article 21.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress in Skill Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a comprehensive review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting significant progress in skill development.
• The report notes increased integration of vocational education into mainstream schooling and higher education, leading to improved employability outcomes.
• Initiatives like the National Credit Framework (NCF) and the Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) have streamlined learning pathways and recognized prior learning.
• Challenges remain in infrastructure development, teacher training, and ensuring equitable access to quality education in remote areas.
• The review suggests further reforms to promote multilingualism, digital literacy, and critical thinking skills across all educational levels.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry —the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 implementation review highlights progress in skill development.
Vocational education is being integrated into mainstream education.
National Credit Framework (NCF) and Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) are key initiatives.
Ministry of Education released the report.
Challenges include infrastructure and teacher training.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key achievements and challenges in the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, particularly concerning skill development.
Discuss how the integration of vocational education and frameworks like NCF and ABC contribute to enhancing employability and lifelong learning.
Evaluate the measures needed to address the existing disparities in access to quality education and infrastructure in remote areas under NEP 2020.
Examine the role of NEP 2020 in fostering a holistic, multidisciplinary, and future-ready education system in India.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaging in a vocational training workshop.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986, which had been in place for 34 years.

articles.headline: Government Introduces New Scheme to Promote Organic Farming and Sustainable Agriculture
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare has launched a new national scheme, 'Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana Plus' (PKVY+), to aggressively promote organic and sustainable farming practices.
• The scheme offers financial incentives to farmers for adopting organic certification, soil health management, and integrated pest management techniques.
• PKVY+ aims to create Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) focused on organic produce, facilitating market linkages and value addition for farmers.
• Emphasis is placed on reducing chemical fertilizer and pesticide use, enhancing soil biodiversity, and promoting climate-resilient agricultural systems.
• The initiative aligns with India's commitments to sustainable development goals and enhancing farmers' income through eco-friendly methods.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System—objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India—scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: PKVY+ (Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana Plus) promotes organic and sustainable farming.
It offers financial incentives for organic certification and soil health management.
Focus on creating Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) for organic produce.
Aims to reduce chemical fertilizer use and enhance soil biodiversity.
Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare is the nodal ministry.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of promoting organic farming and sustainable agriculture in India for food security, environmental health, and farmers' income.
Analyze the features and potential impact of schemes like PKVY+ in encouraging a shift towards eco-friendly farming practices.
Evaluate the role of Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) in strengthening the value chain for organic produce and empowering farmers.
Examine the challenges faced by organic farmers in India and suggest policy measures to overcome them.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer tending to an organic vegetable field.
articles.trivia: Sikkim was declared India's first fully organic state in 2016.

articles.headline: Ancient Rock-Cut Caves in Western Ghats Nominated for UNESCO World Heritage Status
articles.explainer:
• A cluster of ancient rock-cut caves, showcasing remarkable Buddhist, Jain, and early Hindu art and architecture in the Western Ghats region, has been officially nominated for UNESCO World Heritage Site status.
• The nomination highlights the unique cultural, historical, and archaeological significance of these caves, which served as monastic dwellings, prayer halls, and artistic expressions over centuries.
• Conservation efforts have been intensified to protect these fragile sites from environmental degradation and human impact, involving local communities and heritage experts.
• The nomination process requires detailed documentation, site management plans, and demonstrating 'Outstanding Universal Value' as per UNESCO criteria.
• If approved, this designation would bring increased international recognition, funding, and support for the preservation of India's rich rock-cut architectural heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: Ancient rock-cut caves in Western Ghats nominated for UNESCO World Heritage status.
Caves showcase Buddhist, Jain, and early Hindu art and architecture.
UNESCO designation brings international recognition and support for preservation.
The nomination requires demonstrating 'Outstanding Universal Value'.
Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is involved in conservation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of rock-cut architecture in India's cultural heritage, with specific examples from the Western Ghats.
Analyze the challenges involved in the conservation and preservation of ancient archaeological sites and the role of organizations like UNESCO and ASI.
Evaluate the impact of UNESCO World Heritage status on tourism, local economies, and public awareness of cultural heritage.
Examine the 'Outstanding Universal Value' criteria for UNESCO designation and how these caves exemplify them.
articles.imageDescription: An intricate carving inside an ancient rock-cut cave.
articles.trivia: The Ajanta Caves, also in Maharashtra (part of Western Ghats region), were among the first UNESCO World Heritage Sites in India, designated in 1983.

articles.headline: India Develops Indigenous mRNA Vaccine for Emerging Viral Threat
articles.explainer:
• Indian scientists have successfully developed and fast-tracked an indigenous mRNA vaccine targeting a newly identified viral strain with pandemic potential.
• The vaccine, developed through a public-private partnership, utilizes cutting-edge mRNA technology, allowing for rapid development and scalability.
• This achievement marks a significant milestone in India's journey towards self-reliance in vaccine development and public health preparedness.
• Clinical trials have shown promising results in terms of safety and efficacy, paving the way for emergency use authorization.
• The development strengthens India's 'Vaccine Maitri' initiative, positioning the country as a global contributor to health security.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Biotechnology.
prelimsPoints: India developed an indigenous mRNA vaccine for a new viral strain.
mRNA technology allows for rapid vaccine development.
This is a significant step towards self-reliance in vaccine development.
Clinical trials showed promising safety and efficacy.
Contributes to India's 'Vaccine Maitri' initiative.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous mRNA vaccine development for India's public health security and global health leadership.
Analyze the advantages of mRNA vaccine technology over traditional vaccine platforms, especially in responding to emerging viral threats.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and government support in accelerating vaccine research and development in India.
Examine how India's 'Vaccine Maitri' initiative contributes to global health equity and strengthens its soft power.
articles.imageDescription: A scientist in a lab coat holding a vial of vaccine.
articles.trivia: mRNA vaccines work by teaching our cells how to make a protein—or even just a piece of a protein—that triggers an immune response inside our bodies.

articles.headline: Smart Cities Mission Phase II Launched with Focus on Green Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs has officially launched Phase II of the Smart Cities Mission, with an enhanced focus on green infrastructure and climate-resilient urban planning.
• This new phase targets 100 additional cities, emphasizing sustainable mobility, renewable energy integration, water management, and urban biodiversity conservation.
• Phase II aims to build upon the successes and lessons learned from the initial phase, integrating advanced digital solutions for efficient municipal services and citizen engagement.
• Increased funding allocations and incentives for innovative green technologies are central to achieving the mission's objectives.
• The initiative seeks to transform Indian cities into more livable, sustainable, and economically vibrant hubs, addressing rapid urbanization challenges.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Smart Cities Mission Phase II launched, focusing on green infrastructure.
Targets 100 additional cities.
Emphasizes sustainable mobility, renewable energy, and water management.
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is the nodal ministry.
Aims to make cities more livable and sustainable.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and objectives of India's Smart Cities Mission, highlighting the enhanced focus on green infrastructure in Phase II.
Analyze how sustainable urban planning and the integration of digital solutions can address the challenges of rapid urbanization in India.
Evaluate the potential impact of Smart Cities Mission Phase II on environmental sustainability, economic growth, and quality of life in Indian cities.
Examine the role of citizen participation and innovative technologies in the successful implementation of smart city initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic city skyline with green spaces and smart technology elements.
articles.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission was launched by Prime Minister Narendra Modi in 2015, initially selecting 100 cities.

articles.headline: India Calls for Reforms in UN Security Council at General Assembly Session
articles.explainer:
• India's Permanent Representative to the United Nations reiterated India's strong call for comprehensive reforms of the UN Security Council (UNSC) during the ongoing UN General Assembly session.
• India highlighted the anachronistic nature of the current UNSC structure, which does not reflect contemporary geopolitical realities and the growing influence of developing nations.
• The demand includes an expansion of both permanent and non-permanent categories of membership, advocating for India's rightful place as a permanent member.
• India emphasized that a reformed UNSC would be more representative, legitimate, and effective in addressing global peace and security challenges.
• The call for reforms was supported by several like-minded countries, underscoring the broad consensus on the need for modernizing global governance institutions.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India called for comprehensive reforms of the UN Security Council (UNSC).
UNSC has 5 permanent members and 10 non-permanent members.
India advocates for expansion of both permanent and non-permanent categories.
India seeks a permanent seat in the UNSC.
The call for reforms emphasizes representation and effectiveness.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind India's persistent demand for UN Security Council reforms and its aspirations for permanent membership.
Analyze how the current structure of the UNSC reflects outdated geopolitical realities and hinders effective global governance.
Evaluate the arguments for and against the expansion of the UNSC's permanent and non-permanent categories.
Examine the challenges and prospects for achieving comprehensive UN reforms and the role of India in this process.
articles.imageDescription: The United Nations headquarters building in New York.
articles.trivia: India has served eight terms as a non-permanent member of the UN Security Council, most recently in 2021-2022.

articles.headline: India's Q2 GDP Growth Exceeds Projections, Driven by Manufacturing and Services
articles.explainer:
• India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) registered an impressive growth rate of 7.8% in the second quarter (July-September) of 2025, surpassing most expert predictions.
• The robust performance was primarily fueled by strong growth in the manufacturing sector, buoyed by increased industrial output and government investment in infrastructure.
• The services sector, particularly IT, financial services, and hospitality, also contributed significantly to the upward trajectory, indicating a broad-based economic recovery.
• Private consumption showed resilience, supported by improving consumer sentiment and festival demand, while investment picked up pace.
• The positive indicators reinforce India's position as one of the fastest-growing major economies globally, despite prevailing global economic uncertainties.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Investment models.
articles.source: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation
prelimsPoints: India's Q2 (July-September 2025) GDP grew by 7.8%.
Growth primarily driven by manufacturing and services sectors.
Private consumption also showed resilience.
Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation releases GDP data.
India remains one of the fastest-growing major economies.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key drivers behind India's robust GDP growth in Q2 2025 and its implications for the overall economic outlook.
Discuss the role of government policies, investment, and private consumption in sustaining economic momentum in India.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India to maintain high growth rates amidst global economic uncertainties and geopolitical shifts.
Examine the sectoral contributions to GDP growth and the measures needed to ensure inclusive and sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: A graph showing upward trend of economic growth.
articles.trivia: GDP measures the monetary value of all finished goods and services made within a country during a specific period.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Jan Bhagidari' Portal for Citizen Feedback on Development Projects
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government has launched the 'Jan Bhagidari' (Citizen Participation) portal, an online platform designed to solicit direct feedback from citizens on various government development projects.
• The portal aims to enhance transparency, accountability, and citizen engagement in the governance process, allowing individuals to track project progress and submit suggestions or grievances.
• This initiative is part of the broader 'Minimum Government, Maximum Governance' agenda, leveraging digital tools to foster a more responsive and participatory democracy.
• Citizens can access information on projects across different ministries and states, providing feedback that will be directly routed to relevant authorities for action.
• The portal is expected to improve project efficiency, identify bottlenecks, and ensure that development initiatives are aligned with public needs and priorities.
gsSubTopics: Governance, Transparency and Accountability, e-governance applications, models, successes, limitations and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
prelimsPoints: The 'Jan Bhagidari' portal is for citizen feedback on government projects.
Aims to enhance transparency, accountability, and citizen engagement.
Part of the 'Minimum Government, Maximum Governance' initiative.
Citizens can track project progress and submit suggestions/grievances.
Expected to improve project efficiency and alignment with public needs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of digital platforms like 'Jan Bhagidari' in promoting good governance and strengthening participatory democracy in India.
Analyze how technology can enhance transparency and accountability in the implementation of government development projects.
Evaluate the potential benefits and challenges of integrating citizen feedback into public policy and project management.
Examine the 'Minimum Government, Maximum Governance' philosophy and its practical application through e-governance initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a digital portal interface with icons for feedback and project tracking.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'Jan Bhagidari' (people's participation) is a cornerstone of democratic governance and development efforts in India.

articles.headline: Editorial: Vice Presidential Election - A Testament to Democratic Vigour
articles.explainer:
• The recent election of C. P. Radhakrishnan as India's Vice President highlights the robust functioning of India's democratic institutions.
• The electoral process, involving members of both Houses of Parliament, underscores the constitutional mechanisms designed for smooth transitions of power.
• The outcome reflects the current political landscape and the mandate of the elected representatives, emphasizing the importance of parliamentary strength.
• This election serves as a reminder of the Vice President's crucial role as Chairman of the Rajya Sabha, ensuring legislative functionality and upholding parliamentary traditions.
• The editorial calls for constructive cooperation between the executive and legislative branches to address national priorities and uphold constitutional values.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
mainsPoints: Discuss how the Vice Presidential election reinforces the democratic principles and constitutional framework of India.
Analyze the role of the Vice President as the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha in ensuring legislative efficiency and fostering parliamentary decorum.
Evaluate the significance of such elections in reflecting the political mandate and ensuring the smooth functioning of the Union Executive.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for the new Vice President in upholding constitutional values and promoting constructive parliamentary discourse.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of the Indian Parliament building.
articles.trivia: The office of the Vice President in India is modeled on the lines of the American Vice President.

highlights: C. P. Radhakrishnan elected as India's 15th Vice President, reaffirming democratic processes.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5% amidst easing inflation, signaling stability in monetary policy.
India announces enhanced emission targets at G20 Climate Forum, reinforcing climate action commitments.
ISRO successfully tests a Reusable Launch Vehicle prototype, a significant step for cost-effective space access.
Supreme Court upholds Right to Privacy as a fundamental right, emphasizing robust data protection laws.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the election of the Vice President of India, consider the following statements:
1. The Vice President is elected by an electoral college consisting of members of both Houses of Parliament.
2. The election is held in accordance with the system of proportional representation by means of the single transferable vote.
3. The election is conducted by the Election Commission of India.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The Vice President is indeed elected by an electoral college comprising members of both Houses of Parliament. The election procedure follows the system of proportional representation by means of the single transferable vote, and the entire process, including the conduct of the election, is overseen by the Election Commission of India.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about India's Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) is/are correct?
1. NDCs are commitments made under the Kyoto Protocol.
2. India recently pledged enhanced NDCs at the G20 Climate Forum.
3. NDCs include targets for reducing carbon emission intensity and increasing non-fossil fuel energy capacity.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. NDCs (Nationally Determined Contributions) are commitments made under the Paris Agreement, not the Kyoto Protocol. Statements 2 and 3 are correct. India recently announced enhanced NDCs at the G20 Climate Forum, and these targets typically involve specific goals like reducing carbon emission intensity and increasing non-fossil fuel energy capacity by a certain year.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following about the 'Repo Rate' in India:
1. It is the rate at which commercial banks lend money to the Reserve Bank of India.
2. It is a key tool used by the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) to control inflation.
3. A decrease in repo rate generally makes borrowing cheaper for commercial banks.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. Repo rate is the rate at which the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) lends money to commercial banks. The rate at which commercial banks lend to RBI is the reverse repo rate. Statements 2 and 3 are correct. Repo rate is a primary monetary policy tool used by the MPC to manage liquidity and control inflation. A lower repo rate reduces the cost of funds for banks, which can then reduce their lending rates, making borrowing cheaper.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Right to Privacy' in India is protected as an intrinsic part of which Article of the Constitution?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court of India, in the landmark K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017), declared that the Right to Privacy is protected as an intrinsic part of the right to life and personal liberty under Article 21 of the Constitution. It is also recognized as flowing from other fundamental rights under Part III of the Constitution.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a primary objective of the 'Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana Plus' (PKVY+) scheme?
options: (b) Providing direct income support to all farmers
(d) Facilitating market linkages for organic produce
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Providing direct income support to all farmers
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana Plus (PKVY+) scheme primarily focuses on promoting organic and sustainable farming practices, offering financial incentives for organic certification, enhancing soil health and biodiversity, and facilitating market linkages for organic produce, often through Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs). While it aims to enhance farmers' income through eco-friendly methods, providing direct income support to all farmers is not its primary stated objective, which is typically covered by other schemes like PM-KISAN.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the constitutional role and significance of the Vice President of India. In what ways does the recent election of C. P. Radhakrishnan underscore the robustness of India's democratic institutions? (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure. Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
mainsMocks.approach: Define the constitutional role of the VP (Chairman of Rajya Sabha, acting President). Explain the electoral process briefly. Connect the smooth election to democratic principles, rule of law, and peaceful transfer of power. Mention how it reflects parliamentary strength.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss India's commitment to climate action, particularly in light of its enhanced Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) at international forums. What challenges and opportunities lie ahead for India in achieving its ambitious climate targets while ensuring sustainable development? (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment. GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's NDCs and recent enhancements. Explain the dual challenge of development and climate action. Discuss opportunities (renewable energy, green tech, international cooperation) and challenges (financing, technology transfer, energy security, population growth). Conclude with India's leadership role.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's reiteration of the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right necessitates a robust data protection framework. Discuss the essential features that such a law must embody to safeguard citizens' digital rights in India. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Fundamental Rights, Directive Principles and Fundamental Duties. Governance, Transparency and Accountability, e-governance applications, models, successes, limitations and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
mainsMocks.approach: Start with the significance of the Right to Privacy (Article 21, Puttaswamy judgment). Outline essential features of a data protection law: consent, purpose limitation, data minimization, right to be forgotten, accountability of data fiduciaries, data localization, independent regulatory authority, grievance redressal. Mention balancing with state interests and national security.

quickBytes.text: The Vice President of India is the second-highest constitutional office in the country.
The Asia Cup cricket tournament is typically held every two years.
Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY) is a scheme to promote organic farming in India.
Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) are at the heart of the Paris Agreement.
ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program aims to reduce space launch costs.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
India and France have a long-standing strategic partnership established in 1998.
The 'Jan Bhagidari' portal aims to enhance citizen participation in governance.
India's GDP is the sum of market value of all final goods and services produced in a country.
The Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015 to promote sustainable urban development.

GS1.headline: BCCI Announces India Squad for Upcoming 2025 Asia Cup Tournament
GS1.explainer:
• The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) has unveiled the 15-member squad for the 2025 Asia Cup, scheduled to commence on September 28.
• The selection committee emphasized a blend of experienced players and promising young talent, aiming for strong performance in the regional championship.
• The Asia Cup serves as a crucial preparatory tournament for the upcoming ICC Men's T20 World Cup, providing valuable match practice against top Asian teams.
• Key players like Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli, and Jasprit Bumrah are included, alongside new faces expected to make an impact.
• The tournament will be hosted in Sri Lanka, with matches played across Colombo and Kandy, promising exciting contests.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of sports in nation building and promoting cultural exchange.
prelimsPoints: The 2025 Asia Cup is scheduled to begin on September 28.
The tournament is an international cricket competition contested by men's teams from Asia.
The format of the Asia Cup alternates between ODI and T20I depending on the format of the upcoming major ICC event.
BCCI is the governing body for cricket in India.
GS1.imageDescription: Indian cricket team players in a huddle during a practice session.
GS1.trivia: The Asia Cup is the only continental championship in cricket and is typically held every two years.

GS1.headline: Ancient Rock-Cut Caves in Western Ghats Nominated for UNESCO World Heritage Status
GS1.explainer:
• A cluster of ancient rock-cut caves, showcasing remarkable Buddhist, Jain, and early Hindu art and architecture in the Western Ghats region, has been officially nominated for UNESCO World Heritage Site status.
• The nomination highlights the unique cultural, historical, and archaeological significance of these caves, which served as monastic dwellings, prayer halls, and artistic expressions over centuries.
• Conservation efforts have been intensified to protect these fragile sites from environmental degradation and human impact, involving local communities and heritage experts.
• The nomination process requires detailed documentation, site management plans, and demonstrating 'Outstanding Universal Value' as per UNESCO criteria.
• If approved, this designation would bring increased international recognition, funding, and support for the preservation of India's rich rock-cut architectural heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: Ancient rock-cut caves in Western Ghats nominated for UNESCO World Heritage status.
Caves showcase Buddhist, Jain, and early Hindu art and architecture.
UNESCO designation brings international recognition and support for preservation.
The nomination requires demonstrating 'Outstanding Universal Value'.
Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is involved in conservation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of rock-cut architecture in India's cultural heritage, with specific examples from the Western Ghats.
Analyze the challenges involved in the conservation and preservation of ancient archaeological sites and the role of organizations like UNESCO and ASI.
Evaluate the impact of UNESCO World Heritage status on tourism, local economies, and public awareness of cultural heritage.
Examine the 'Outstanding Universal Value' criteria for UNESCO designation and how these caves exemplify them.
GS1.imageDescription: An intricate carving inside an ancient rock-cut cave.
GS1.trivia: The Ajanta Caves, also in Maharashtra (part of Western Ghats region), were among the first UNESCO World Heritage Sites in India, designated in 1983.

GS2.headline: C. P. Radhakrishnan Elected India's New Vice President
GS2.explainer:
• C. P. Radhakrishnan, former Governor of Jharkhand, has been elected as the 15th Vice President of India, securing a significant majority in the electoral college.
• The election involved members of both Houses of Parliament, with Rajya Sabha Secretary-General serving as the Returning Officer.
• The Vice President holds the constitutional office of Chairman of the Rajya Sabha and acts as the President in case of a vacancy.
• Radhakrishnan's victory marks a significant political development, consolidating the ruling party's position in key constitutional offices.
• His tenure is expected to focus on parliamentary decorum and fostering constructive debates within the upper house.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: C. P. Radhakrishnan is the 15th Vice President of India.
The Vice President is elected by members of an electoral college consisting of members of both Houses of Parliament.
The election is held by secret ballot, using the system of proportional representation by means of the single transferable vote.
The Vice President is ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.
Article 63 to 71 of the Indian Constitution deal with the office of the Vice President.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional role and significance of the Vice President of India in the parliamentary system.
Analyze the electoral process for the Vice President and its distinction from the Presidential election.
Evaluate the potential impact of the new Vice President on the functioning of the Rajya Sabha and inter-house relations.
Examine the powers and functions of the Vice President, particularly as the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha and acting President.
GS2.imageDescription: C. P. Radhakrishnan taking the oath of office as Vice President.
GS2.trivia: The first Vice President of India, Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan, served two terms, from 1952 to 1962.

GS2.headline: New 'PM-Suraksha Awas Yojana' Launched to Boost Affordable Housing
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Government has launched the 'PM-Suraksha Awas Yojana' (PMSAY) aimed at providing affordable and quality housing for all eligible urban and rural poor.
• The scheme integrates various existing housing initiatives and introduces new incentives for private sector participation in affordable housing projects.
• Key features include interest subvention on home loans, direct financial assistance for construction, and promotion of sustainable building technologies.
• PMSAY targets particularly vulnerable groups including EWS, LIG, and those living in informal settlements, with a focus on women beneficiaries.
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs will oversee the implementation, working with state governments and local bodies to achieve housing targets by 2030.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: PM-Suraksha Awas Yojana (PMSAY) aims to provide affordable housing.
The scheme covers both urban and rural poor.
It includes provisions for interest subvention and direct financial assistance.
The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is the nodal ministry.
Focus on sustainable building technologies and women beneficiaries.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of affordable housing schemes like PMSAY in achieving inclusive growth and reducing urban poverty.
Discuss the challenges in implementing large-scale housing projects in India and suggest measures for effective delivery.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and technological innovations in accelerating housing construction under such schemes.
Examine how PMSAY addresses the housing needs of vulnerable sections and promotes gender equality in home ownership.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting affordable housing units under a government scheme.
GS2.trivia: India aims to achieve 'Housing for All' by a specific target year, which has been a recurring goal in national policies.

GS2.headline: India and France Deepen Strategic Partnership with New Defence & Tech Pacts
GS2.explainer:
• India and France have signed a series of agreements to further strengthen their strategic partnership, with a focus on defense, space, and emerging technologies.
• The pacts include joint development projects in advanced defense manufacturing, collaboration in quantum computing, and enhanced maritime security cooperation in the Indo-Pacific.
• This deepening of ties underscores the shared vision of both nations for a stable, secure, and prosperous global order, particularly in the face of evolving geopolitical challenges.
• Both leaders emphasized the importance of multilateralism and adherence to international law, reiterating their commitment to addressing global issues like climate change and terrorism.
• The agreements are expected to boost bilateral trade, investment, and technological exchange, fostering innovation and economic growth.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and France signed new agreements on defence, space, and technology.
Cooperation focuses on advanced defence manufacturing and quantum computing.
Maritime security in the Indo-Pacific is a key area of collaboration.
France is a key strategic partner for India.
Both nations uphold multilateralism and international law.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of the India-France strategic partnership and its significance in India's foreign policy.
Discuss the key areas of cooperation between India and France, particularly in defence, space, and emerging technologies, and their mutual benefits.
Evaluate the role of this partnership in promoting stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region amidst geopolitical shifts.
Examine how bilateral ties with France contribute to India's technological advancement and strategic autonomy.
GS2.imageDescription: Prime Minister of India and President of France shaking hands after signing agreements.
GS2.trivia: India and France established a strategic partnership in 1998, a unique relationship among India's global partners.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Privacy as Fundamental Right in Data Protection Case
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India has once again affirmed the Right to Privacy as an intrinsic part of the fundamental rights guaranteed under Article 21 of the Constitution, in a significant data protection case.
• The ruling emphasizes the need for a robust data protection framework that balances individual privacy with legitimate state interests and technological advancements.
• This judgment reinforces the principles laid down in the landmark Puttaswamy judgment, providing further clarity on the scope and limitations of privacy in the digital age.
• The court directed the government to expedite the enactment of a comprehensive data protection law, ensuring data fiduciary accountability and individual consent mechanisms.
• The decision has wide-ranging implications for digital governance, surveillance practices, and the collection and processing of personal data by both state and private entities.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Fundamental Rights, Directive Principles and Fundamental Duties.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court affirmed Right to Privacy as part of fundamental rights under Article 21.
The ruling reinforces the Puttaswamy judgment (2017).
The court called for a robust data protection law.
It emphasizes individual consent and data fiduciary accountability.
Implications for digital governance and surveillance practices.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right in India and the significance of the latest Supreme Court ruling in this context.
Analyze the challenges in balancing individual privacy rights with legitimate state interests and the demands of national security in the digital era.
Evaluate the essential features of a comprehensive data protection law for India and how it can safeguard citizens' digital rights.
Examine the impact of such judicial pronouncements on digital governance, surveillance, and the responsibilities of data fiduciaries.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal books, symbolizing judicial pronouncements.
GS2.trivia: The K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared that the Right to Privacy is protected as an intrinsic part of the right to life and personal liberty under Article 21.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress in Skill Development
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a comprehensive review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting significant progress in skill development.
• The report notes increased integration of vocational education into mainstream schooling and higher education, leading to improved employability outcomes.
• Initiatives like the National Credit Framework (NCF) and the Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) have streamlined learning pathways and recognized prior learning.
• Challenges remain in infrastructure development, teacher training, and ensuring equitable access to quality education in remote areas.
• The review suggests further reforms to promote multilingualism, digital literacy, and critical thinking skills across all educational levels.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry —the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 implementation review highlights progress in skill development.
Vocational education is being integrated into mainstream education.
National Credit Framework (NCF) and Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) are key initiatives.
Ministry of Education released the report.
Challenges include infrastructure and teacher training.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key achievements and challenges in the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, particularly concerning skill development.
Discuss how the integration of vocational education and frameworks like NCF and ABC contribute to enhancing employability and lifelong learning.
Evaluate the measures needed to address the existing disparities in access to quality education and infrastructure in remote areas under NEP 2020.
Examine the role of NEP 2020 in fostering a holistic, multidisciplinary, and future-ready education system in India.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaging in a vocational training workshop.
GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986, which had been in place for 34 years.

GS2.headline: India Calls for Reforms in UN Security Council at General Assembly Session
GS2.explainer:
• India's Permanent Representative to the United Nations reiterated India's strong call for comprehensive reforms of the UN Security Council (UNSC) during the ongoing UN General Assembly session.
• India highlighted the anachronistic nature of the current UNSC structure, which does not reflect contemporary geopolitical realities and the growing influence of developing nations.
• The demand includes an expansion of both permanent and non-permanent categories of membership, advocating for India's rightful place as a permanent member.
• India emphasized that a reformed UNSC would be more representative, legitimate, and effective in addressing global peace and security challenges.
• The call for reforms was supported by several like-minded countries, underscoring the broad consensus on the need for modernizing global governance institutions.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India called for comprehensive reforms of the UN Security Council (UNSC).
UNSC has 5 permanent members and 10 non-permanent members.
India advocates for expansion of both permanent and non-permanent categories.
India seeks a permanent seat in the UNSC.
The call for reforms emphasizes representation and effectiveness.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind India's persistent demand for UN Security Council reforms and its aspirations for permanent membership.
Analyze how the current structure of the UNSC reflects outdated geopolitical realities and hinders effective global governance.
Evaluate the arguments for and against the expansion of the UNSC's permanent and non-permanent categories.
Examine the challenges and prospects for achieving comprehensive UN reforms and the role of India in this process.
GS2.imageDescription: The United Nations headquarters building in New York.
GS2.trivia: India has served eight terms as a non-permanent member of the UN Security Council, most recently in 2021-2022.

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'Jan Bhagidari' Portal for Citizen Feedback on Development Projects
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Government has launched the 'Jan Bhagidari' (Citizen Participation) portal, an online platform designed to solicit direct feedback from citizens on various government development projects.
• The portal aims to enhance transparency, accountability, and citizen engagement in the governance process, allowing individuals to track project progress and submit suggestions or grievances.
• This initiative is part of the broader 'Minimum Government, Maximum Governance' agenda, leveraging digital tools to foster a more responsive and participatory democracy.
• Citizens can access information on projects across different ministries and states, providing feedback that will be directly routed to relevant authorities for action.
• The portal is expected to improve project efficiency, identify bottlenecks, and ensure that development initiatives are aligned with public needs and priorities.
gsSubTopics: Governance, Transparency and Accountability, e-governance applications, models, successes, limitations and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
prelimsPoints: The 'Jan Bhagidari' portal is for citizen feedback on government projects.
Aims to enhance transparency, accountability, and citizen engagement.
Part of the 'Minimum Government, Maximum Governance' initiative.
Citizens can track project progress and submit suggestions/grievances.
Expected to improve project efficiency and alignment with public needs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of digital platforms like 'Jan Bhagidari' in promoting good governance and strengthening participatory democracy in India.
Analyze how technology can enhance transparency and accountability in the implementation of government development projects.
Evaluate the potential benefits and challenges of integrating citizen feedback into public policy and project management.
Examine the 'Minimum Government, Maximum Governance' philosophy and its practical application through e-governance initiatives.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a digital portal interface with icons for feedback and project tracking.
GS2.trivia: The concept of 'Jan Bhagidari' (people's participation) is a cornerstone of democratic governance and development efforts in India.

GS2.headline: Editorial: Vice Presidential Election - A Testament to Democratic Vigour
GS2.explainer:
• The recent election of C. P. Radhakrishnan as India's Vice President highlights the robust functioning of India's democratic institutions.
• The electoral process, involving members of both Houses of Parliament, underscores the constitutional mechanisms designed for smooth transitions of power.
• The outcome reflects the current political landscape and the mandate of the elected representatives, emphasizing the importance of parliamentary strength.
• This election serves as a reminder of the Vice President's crucial role as Chairman of the Rajya Sabha, ensuring legislative functionality and upholding parliamentary traditions.
• The editorial calls for constructive cooperation between the executive and legislative branches to address national priorities and uphold constitutional values.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
mainsPoints: Discuss how the Vice Presidential election reinforces the democratic principles and constitutional framework of India.
Analyze the role of the Vice President as the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha in ensuring legislative efficiency and fostering parliamentary decorum.
Evaluate the significance of such elections in reflecting the political mandate and ensuring the smooth functioning of the Union Executive.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for the new Vice President in upholding constitutional values and promoting constructive parliamentary discourse.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized image of the Indian Parliament building.
GS2.trivia: The office of the Vice President in India is modeled on the lines of the American Vice President.

GS3.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Emission Targets at G20 Climate Forum
GS3.explainer:
• India has announced a significant enhancement of its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) at the G20 Climate and Environment Ministers' Meeting.
• The revised targets include a more ambitious reduction in carbon emission intensity and a substantial increase in non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
• The commitment underscores India's dedication to global climate action while balancing its development imperatives and energy security needs.
• India called for greater financial and technological support from developed nations to assist developing countries in their climate mitigation and adaptation efforts.
• Discussions also focused on collective efforts towards achieving net-zero emissions, climate finance, and technology transfer mechanisms.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: India updated its NDCs at the G20 Climate Forum.
NDCs are national climate action plans under the Paris Agreement.
The new targets include reducing carbon emission intensity and increasing non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
G20 is an international forum for the governments and central bank governors from 19 countries and the European Union.
India emphasizes common but differentiated responsibilities and respective capabilities (CBDR-RC).
mainsPoints: Analyze India's enhanced NDCs in the context of global climate action and its implications for sustainable development.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in transitioning to a low-carbon economy, considering its energy security needs.
Evaluate the role of international forums like G20 in fostering climate cooperation and the need for equitable climate finance.
Examine the concept of 'common but differentiated responsibilities' (CBDR-RC) and its relevance in current climate negotiations.
GS3.imageDescription: Ministers from G20 nations attending a climate discussion, with flags in the background.
GS3.trivia: The Paris Agreement aims to limit global warming to well below 2, preferably to 1.5 degrees Celsius, compared to pre-industrial levels.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Prototype
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has successfully conducted a critical test flight of its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
• This test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating the return of a spacecraft from orbit, a key step towards fully reusable launch systems.
• RLV technology aims to significantly reduce the cost of space access by making rockets reusable, similar to aircraft.
• The development is crucial for India's ambitious future space missions, including human spaceflight and commercial satellite launches.
• ISRO is focusing on indigenization of advanced space technologies to enhance India's self-reliance in the global space industry.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested a Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
RLV technology aims to reduce space launch costs by making rockets reusable.
The test involved a controlled landing experiment.
ISRO is India's national space agency.
This technology is vital for future missions like Gaganyaan (human spaceflight).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its economic implications.
Analyze how indigenization of advanced space technologies contributes to India's self-reliance and global standing in the space sector.
Evaluate the challenges and future prospects of developing fully reusable launch systems and their potential impact on space exploration.
Examine the role of ISRO in driving technological innovation and its contribution to national development.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic illustration of a Reusable Launch Vehicle descending for landing.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's first experimental RLV, the Hypersonic Flight Experiment (HEX), was successfully tested in 2016.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive meeting.
• This decision comes as retail inflation shows signs of moderation, moving closer to the RBI's comfort zone, supported by favorable monsoon and supply-side measures.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Economic growth projections remain robust, with expectations of sustained momentum in manufacturing and services sectors.
• Analysts suggest this pause allows the RBI to assess the durability of disinflation and global economic uncertainties before making further moves.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC kept the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
The decision is based on easing retail inflation.
MPC consists of six members (three from RBI, three external).
The primary objective of monetary policy is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for inflation and economic growth.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee in balancing inflation control with the objective of supporting economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in managing inflationary pressures and ensuring financial stability in the Indian economy.
Examine how global economic factors and domestic supply-side dynamics influence the RBI's monetary policy decisions.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) logo with currency notes in the background.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 to bring transparency and accountability in formulating monetary policy.

GS3.headline: Integrated Border Management System Pilot Project Launched in Northeast
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs has launched a pilot project for an Integrated Border Management System (IBMS) along a vulnerable stretch of India's northeastern border.
• The system employs advanced surveillance technologies, including drones, thermal imagers, ground sensors, and radar, to enhance real-time monitoring and threat detection.
• The initiative aims to curb illegal infiltration, smuggling, and cross-border crimes, bolstering national security and border integrity.
• IBMS integrates data from various sources into a common operating picture, enabling quicker response times and better coordination among border guarding forces.
• This pilot project will be evaluated for scalability and potential rollout across other challenging border areas of the country.
